title: Session opener: resume sessions in same or new terminal
description:
Implement the opener package that handles opening/resuming a Claude Code session by cd-ing into the project directory and running claude --resume.

Refer to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-13-ccs-design.md for the opening mechanism.

This package depends on internal/model from task ccs-01-foundation.
requirements: Create internal/opener/opener.go with OpenSession(session model.Session, newTerminal bool) error function
Same-terminal mode (default): use exec.Command to run 'bash -c "cd <cwd> && claude --resume <sessionId>"' with stdin/stdout/stderr inherited
New-terminal mode (--new-terminal): spawn a detached process with SysProcAttr Setsid=true
Before opening, check if session.Cwd exists on disk. If not, return a descriptive error suggesting the user open in current dir instead

Create internal/opener/resolve.go with ResolveSession(db *db.DB, identifier string) (model.Session, error) that resolves an identifier by: exact UUID match, UUID prefix (4+ chars), exact name match, fuzzy name match. Return error if no match or ambiguous (include list of candidates in error message)
Add tests in internal/opener/resolve_test.go for the resolution logic (mock or use test DB)
Verify the project compiles with 'go build ./cmd/ccs/' and all tests pass with 'go test ./...'
